opp.name: MTR_HK_CLM
opp.next_step: 30% confidence Jul 7th Spoke to Lillian- she wanted to have genral discussion and opportunity to ask anything befor RFP submission tomorrow. She asked about SI, andh hoped we could do implementation ourself- wants BEST price to win. She agreed I could meet team next week F2F in HK if I wanted. Confirmed eval will take 1.5 mths and award is most likely mid October Jun 9th RFP has come out today- ck to reach out to project lead- and May 04: Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities. KIV and get update by July Apr27 No update on date of Tender. Chasing after sourcing project manager. Apr13 RFP to be received by April 20. Pending session on Risk management with procurement Mar 27 just re confirm Tender will come early April - decision to be made by June Mar 13- Meeting Procurement COE head next week- Tender still expected by March Feb 09 - some administrative clarification requested - Tender by March - execs meetings scheduled for RT by March 18 Jan 19th Tender will be finally shared in March. Final decision expected by Q1 Jan 12th: Confirmation short listed. Tender to be received by mid Jan (partnering with EY). Vendor selected by March. Dec 08th Project on hold until Jan end. Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement and missing clarity on next steps. Nov 24th: Meeting with Business owner in Nov 26: need to clarify next steps and timeline 05th Nov: HoP confirms process is moving slowly and Zycus to be short listed. EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate). Risk of delay to Q4. 31st Oct: Still some clarifications to be responded. Meeting with CPO next week. Challenging for Q3 closing. 13rd Oct: Tender is now expected by mid Nov 03rd Oct: process is going slow. Add clarification on configuration responded this week. Good traction with sourcing team so far. 22nd Sep: Working with EY to get exec engagement. Tender expected in coming 2 weeks 12nd Sep: Add clarification made. Tender expected by End Sep. F2F by Oct (PM & Exec sponsor) 01st Sep: Add clarification on implementation & maintenance requested. Still pending of Tender 18th Aug: Tender invitation is expected by mid Sep 01st Aug: AI clarification responded. Chasing for a F2F meeting by beg of Sep 25th Jul: Add' clarification on pricing shared + respond to AI clarification by Aug 01 21st Jul: Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01) 15th Jul: Good overview session yesterday. Customer will come back in 1 month to clarify short list 14th Jul: Financial shared on Fri11. Use cases demo on Jul14 07th Jul: Short listed. Demo by July 14 30th Jun: Demo to happen by mid-July 23rd Jun: Pending confirmation demos date. 16th Jun: RFI responses sent on Jun12. Use case demo under scheduling by beg of July. 09th Jun: RFI for CLM received. To be responded by Jun12. Flanking approach on bundling both eAuction & CLM opportunities. 19th May: Demo well received. Ask for a first $ estimation. 28th Apr: eAuction demo by May 13. NDA signature going on. 18th Apr: eAuction/ Sourcing Demo expected by May02. Process on going 10th Apr: NDA to be signed. Validate eAuction POC feasibility (paid POC). POC to be delivered in coming 2 months. 04th Apr: Meeting by Apr 10 to clarify use case around eAuction and next steps. 03rd Mar: Focus on migrating some Oracle EBS proc modules this year. In parallel, they want/must start with GenAI use case. Working with Arcblue on "BPR". Invitation to Horizon. Call back in 1 month. 17th Feb: F2F meeting on Feb 27 to align on next steps 27th Jan: Request for a F2F meeting by end of Feb 17th Dec: Qualification & Overview demo. Still early stage. Expect to meet in HK by end of Jan.
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 21 Jul 2025 rep note: 'Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01).' Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' This is a verbatim buyer-side AI-clarification request as part of the formal CLM tender ‚Äî MTR's procurement team explicitly requested additional information about Zycus's AI capabilities during the evaluation cycle.
• On 4 May 2026: 'Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities. KIV and get update by July.' On 19 Sep 2024: 'Update from On Wing next week (confirmation if they open a selection process).' MTR is a long-running multi-stage evaluation (~14 months captured) bundling CLM with eAuction/Sourcing. The AI-info clarification was a real, dated, buyer-originated AI question ‚Äî but the project is currently frozen.
• Confidence is Medium. MTR has only Merlin in product scope (single AI SKU bundled with iContract for CLM) and the AI signal is the formal info-request rather than a champion-led AI demand. Project is frozen as of May 2026. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric) reflects genuine buyer AI-clarification ask without an active AI-led evaluation.

contacts.name: Simon Fung
contacts.title: Assistant Manager- Data Analytics & Biz Sys

contacts.name: Navjot Singh
contacts.title: Procurement Head - Centre of Excellence, Procurement Transformation

items.quote: Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement and missing clarity on next steps.
Demo well received. Ask for a first $ estimation.

metrics.narrative: MTR articulated qualitative pain (migrating Oracle EBS procurement modules and wanting to start a GenAI use case, working with Arcblue on business process re-engineering; SF Next_Step, 3 Mar 2025) and asked Zycus for 'a first $ estimation' after the 13 May 2025 eAuction demo (SF Next_Step, 19 May 2025), but no quantified savings, cost reduction, or ROI figure has ever been delivered back or discussed in any subsequent log entry through the 9 Jun 2026 RFP issuance. Quantifying that estimate remains the single biggest open ask.

champion.narrative: No champion or executive sponsor has been identified: the Next_Step log states explicitly 'Not able to identify champion/exec sponsor engagement' as of 8 Dec 2025, and no later entry reverses that. Simon Fung and Chauncy Chow are the recurring working-level contacts across the three content-bearing calls (27 Feb, 10 Apr, 13 May 2025) but neither has been confirmed as an internal advocate driving the tender internally or pushing back on EY's iCertis preference.

competition.narrative: iCertis is the reconciled, current strongest threat: EY, MTR's tender advisory partner, has pushed for iCertis since at least 5 Nov 2025, and Zycus is 'working with Alliance to mitigate' with no evidence of success logged since. Sirion Labs is also listed on the Competitor field but has no corroborating mention in any call, Next_Step entry, or task, making it a materially lower and unconfirmed current threat by comparison.

identify_pain.narrative: MTR's stated pain spans two threads: an operational/CLM-adjacent pain around migrating Oracle EBS procurement modules while wanting to start a GenAI use case (SF Next_Step, 3 Mar 2025, working with partner Arcblue on 'BPR'), and a functional pain in the eAuction process where a buyer-side speaker said 'there is a need for transparency in the auction process, but buyers should not be required to monitor it as closely as the stock market' (Avoma E-Auction Demo, 13 May 2025). The pain owner for the CLM thread is the procurement/sourcing team; no single named pain owner has been confirmed beyond that.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) have been discussed for the CLM opportunity in any source; the deal is still at RFP-scoring stage. The one paper-process precedent on record is an NDA signed for a related eAuction POC in Apr 2025, which does not extend to CLM contracting. Visibility here would come from tracking what MTR's procurement/legal team requires once the RFP scoring concludes.

economic_buyer.narrative: Alex Gautier, General Manager - Procurement & Supply Chain, is named as Decision Maker on the OpportunityContactRole (agautier@mtr.com.hk), but he does not appear as an attendee on any of the 4 Avoma-discovered calls spanning Feb 2025 to Jul 2025, nor in any later Next_Step entry naming a direct meeting with him. A 'Meeting with CPO next week' was logged 31 Oct 2025 but no outcome or attendee was ever confirmed in subsequent notes. Budget authority remains unreached.

decision_process.narrative: The process is traceable end to end in the Next_Step log: RFI (Jun 2025) -> use-case demos -> shortlisting confirmed 7 Jul 2025 -> a year-long clarification and exec-engagement cycle -> a project freeze (~4 May 2026) -> formal RFP issuance 9 Jun 2026 (PQ114354). EY, an external advisory partner, supports MTR's tender process and has been pushing for iCertis since 5 Nov 2025; MTR's Procurement Centre of Excellence head (Navjot Singh) was also due to be met around 13 Mar 2026. Vendor selection was previously targeted for March 2026 (12 Jan 2026 entry) but slipped to the RFP stage instead.

decision_criteria.narrative: MTR ran a formal RFI for CLM (received 9 Jun 2025, responded 12 Jun 2025) and has raised discrete clarification rounds covering AI/GenAI capability (21 Jul 2025, responded 1 Aug 2025), pricing (25 Jul 2025), implementation and maintenance (1 Sep 2025), configuration (3 Oct 2025), and is now pending a risk-management session with procurement (13 Apr 2026). These rounds outline the real evaluation criteria even though MTR has not shared a consolidated scoring rubric.

items.name: Carl requests bank transfer option for digital certificate fee
items.summary: Carl Kimball asked MTR's Kenneth Wong whether Zycus could pay the mandatory HKD150 e-tender digital certificate fee via bank transfer rather than cheque, framing it as a final registration step ahead of the 8 Jul tender submission.

items.name: Kenneth Wong provides HSBC bank details
items.summary: Kenneth Wong (MTR Procurement) sent HSBC account and SWIFT details for the fee payment and asked Zycus to remit HKD150 and send back a remittance notice for reconciliation.

items.name: Carl confirms remittance in progress
items.summary: Carl replied confirming Zycus would wire the fee that evening and would follow up with remittance details — closing the loop on this registration prerequisite just before the tender deadline.

items.name: Lilian re-sends tender clarification notice (Q114354)
items.summary: Lilian Au-Yeung resent the CLMS tender clarification email (after a technical issue with the earlier version), reconfirming the submission deadline of 8 Jul 2026 14:00 HKT and asking bidders to provide a senior management contact for a message MTR's leadership wants to share; Carl responded naming himself as VP Asia Pacific & Japan.

day_summary.overall: With the tender deadline just two days out (8 Jul), Carl personally chased down a last logistical hurdle — getting the HKD150 digital certificate fee paid via bank transfer instead of cheque — and MTR's Kenneth Wong promptly supplied HSBC account details, which Carl confirmed would be remitted same evening. In parallel, MTR's procurement lead Lilian Au-Yeung resent the tender clarification notice (fixing a technical glitch) reconfirming the 8 Jul 14:00 HKT deadline and requesting Zycus's senior management contact for a message MTR wants to deliver to bidders, to which Carl responded with his own name/title as the executive contact. Ball is now in Zycus's court to complete the payment and submit the tender on time; MTR is waiting on the remittance notice and the exec contact confirmation.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.read: Accelerating — one of the hotter deals in the book.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: 11 inbound buyer repl(ies) in the last 30d (legacy volume read)
buyer touch 2d ago — within the ~18d cadence this stage needs
10 upcoming milestone(s), nearest 14 Jul 2026 — live advancing plan

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: iCertis is the clear and current strongest threat, actively championed by EY (MTR's tender advisory partner) since at least 5 Nov 2025, with no evidence since that Zycus's Alliance-channel mitigation has succeeded.

bullets.full: No champion identified. The Next_Step log states 'Not able to identify champion/exec sponsor engagement' as of 2025-12-08. Without an internal advocate, Zycus has no counterweight to EY's iCertis preference, and the tender decision will be driven by the advisory partner rather than an internal sponsor.

The close date (2026-12-18, 165 days out) is unrealistic. The RFP was issued 2026-06-09 (4 weeks ago), and a typical enterprise tender timeline (RFP response, evaluation, negotiation, contract, signature) requires 5 to 7 months. No vendor-selection date has been confirmed by MTR, and the Next_Step shows 30% confidence.
bullets.text: The close date (18 Dec 2026, 165 days out) is unrealistic.

items: Confirm the RFP response deadline and evaluation timeline directly with MTR's project lead. (by 14 Jun)
Have Shekhar Varma request a direct commercial conversation with Alex Gautier, the unengaged decision maker. (by 21 Jun)
Deliver a quantified dollar estimate answering MTR's outstanding 'first dollar estimation' ask. (by 28 Jun)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: MTR Corporation Limited — Shortlisted · $260K · closes 18 Sep

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 60 is carried by momentum 79 vs stage-expected 56; differentiation strength +1.00 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'manual process' in…. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 79 (moving forward): 11 inbound buyer repl(ies) in the last 30d (legacy volume read). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 53 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 57 is anchored on the stage win (60), nudged by commitment 53 and momentum 79, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: differentiation strength +1.00 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'manual process' in Next-Step/narrative
exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'cpo' in Next-Step/narrative
Win capped at 60: champion not established (gap) — a higher win probability has to be earned by qualifying this first

Independent of AIS, MTR has surfaced genuine AI interest multiple times: wanting to 'start a GenAI use case' alongside an Oracle EBS migration (Next_Step, 3 Mar 2025) and requesting AI clarification twice (21 Jul 2025, responded 1 Aug 2025).

deal_movement.summary: The deal has been live for roughly 13 months since creation (13 Jun 2025) and closer to 18 months of actual engagement (first call 27 Feb 2025), moving through RFI, demos, and shortlisting in 2025, then stalling with a project freeze around 4 May 2026, before restarting with the formal RFP (PQ114354) on 9 Jun 2026. The pulse shows genuine, current buyer engagement: MTR's procurement team replied by email on 6 Jul 2026, a day after Carl Kimball's follow-up on the RFP.

items.detail: EY (advisory partner supporting MTR's tender process) is pushing for iCertis, creating a significant headwind. Zycus is 'working with Alliance to mitigate' but there is no evidence the mitigation has succeeded or that MTR has overruled EY's preference.

No Economic Buyer engagement. Alex Gautier (GM Procurement, Decision Maker) is mapped but has not been engaged in any call, pricing discussion, or ROI conversation. No CFO, CPO, or C-level budget authority has been named or engaged.

No champion identified. The Next_Step log states 'Not able to identify champion/exec sponsor engagement' as of 2025-12-08. Without an internal advocate, Zycus has no counterweight to EY's iCertis preference, and the tender decision will be driven by the advisory partner rather than an internal sponsor.

The close date (2026-12-18, 165 days out) is unrealistic. The RFP was issued 2026-06-09 (4 weeks ago), and a typical enterprise tender timeline (RFP response, evaluation, negotiation, contract, signature) requires 5 to 7 months. No vendor-selection date has been confirmed by MTR, and the Next_Step shows 30% confidence.

No quantified ROI or business case. No buyer has stated savings targets, cost reduction, or quantified value. MTR requested a 'first dollar estimation' after the May 2025 demo, but no ROI workshop or commercial justification has been logged.
Project was frozen in May 2026 due to internal MTR priorities and only resumed with the RFP in June. This signals competing internal initiatives and potential for further delay or de-prioritization.
EY, MTR's tender advisory partner, has pushed for iCertis since 5 Nov 2025; Zycus is working through its Alliance partnership to mitigate but no evidence of success has been logged since.

items.detail: Alex Gautier (mapped Decision Maker, GM Procurement) has never attended a Zycus call, and no champion or exec sponsor has been identified (Next_Step, 8 Dec 2025), leaving Zycus without a counterweight to EY's recommendation.

The 18 Dec 2026 close date sits against a 30%-confidence self-rating and a project freeze that only lifted with the 9 Jun 2026 RFP issuance, leaving roughly 6 months for RFP scoring, vendor selection, and contracting.
No quantified ROI or business case has been delivered since MTR requested 'a first $ estimation' on 19 May 2025.

items.expected_effect: Sharpens the RFP response to directly counter the two named rivals.

confidence_signals.summary: MTR's own Next_Step log self-rates confidence at 30% as of 7 Jul 2026, the most direct confidence signal available; the recent email reply (6 Jul 2026) is a positive counter-signal after the earlier project freeze.

north_star_verdict.math: 164 days to close (2026-12-18). Opportunity created 2025-06-13 (~13 months in pipeline). RFP (PQ114354) issued 2026-06-09, the real evaluation-clock start; MTR's Next_Step self-rates confidence at 30% as of 2026-07-07. Deal was frozen ~2026-05-04 for internal MTR priorities before resuming with the RFP. A 2026-12-18 close requires RFP scoring, vendor selection, and contracting to complete inside ~6 months from a standing restart, against MTR's demonstrated pattern of multi-month gaps between milestones (e.g. Jul 2025 shortlist to Jun 2026 RFP is an ~11-month gap).
evidence: SF Next_Step: 'May 04: Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities' (2026-05-04)
SF Next_Step: 'Jun 9th RFP has come out today' (2026-06-09)
SF Next_Step: 'Dec 08th ... Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement' (2025-12-08)
north_star_verdict.headline: RFP for CLM issued 9 Jun 2026 (~4 weeks ago) after a Salesforce-logged project freeze around 4 May 2026; MTR's own confidence rating is 30%, and the mapped decision maker (Alex Gautier) has never engaged Zycus directly across 18 months.

flags: Counter EY's iCertis preference with an AI/Merlin differentiation deep-dive led by Navjot Singh (Procurement Transformation lead) and Alex Gautier (GM Procurement), positioning Zycus as the AI-native CLM solution that enables MTR's stated GenAI transformation goal.
Develop Navjot Singh or Chauncy Chow as a champion by coaching them on Zycus's AI capabilities and securing their advocacy in the internal tender evaluation and against EY's iCertis recommendation.

Secure Economic Buyer engagement with Alex Gautier (GM Procurement, Decision Maker) to discuss commercials, ROI, and budget approval, anchoring the business case in MTR's Oracle EBS migration and procurement transformation timeline.

EY, MTR's tender advisory partner, favors iCertis (SF Next_Step, 5 Nov 2025); push the Zycus-Alliance channel for direct visibility into EY's scoring criteria since no internal champion currently counters that recommendation.

Alex Gautier, the mapped GM Procurement decision maker, has never attended a Zycus call in 18 months; multi-thread the evaluation team to reach him directly rather than relying solely on Simon Fung and Chauncy Chow.
best_practice_check.summary: Two structural gaps (unengaged economic buyer, no champion) sit underneath a real but fragile competitive position against an EY-backed iCertis push.

competitive_position.summary: iCertis is the clear and current strongest threat, actively championed by EY (MTR's tender advisory partner) since at least 5 Nov 2025, with no evidence since that Zycus's Alliance-channel mitigation has succeeded. Sirion Labs is also listed in the Competitor field but has no corroborating mention in any call, Next_Step entry, or task, making it a materially lower, unconfirmed threat by comparison.

competitors.quote: EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate). Risk of delay to Q4.
competitors.how_we_win: Escalate through the Zycus-Alliance partnership with EY for direct visibility into evaluation criteria, and get Alex Gautier a direct, unfiltered view of Zycus's AI/Merlin capability.

competitors.name: Sirion Labs
competitors.how_we_win: No corroborating call or note evidence yet; monitor for a live mention before prioritizing a direct counter.

Feb 09 - some administrative clarification requested - Tender by March - execs meetings scheduled for RT by March 18
Jan 19th Tender will be finally shared in March. Final decision expected by Q1
Jan 12th: Confirmation short listed. Tender to be received by mid Jan (partnering with EY). Vendor selected by March.
Dec 08th Project on hold until Jan end. Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement and missing clarity on next steps.
Nov 24th: Meeting with Business owner in Nov 26: need to clarify next steps and timeline

05th Nov: HoP confirms process is moving slowly and Zycus to be short listed. EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate). Risk of delay to Q4.

31st Oct: Still some clarifications to be responded. Meeting with CPO next week. Challenging for Q3 closing.

13rd Oct: Tender is now expected by mid Nov

03rd Oct: process is going slow. Add clarification on configuration responded this week. Good traction with sourcing team so far.

22nd Sep: Working with EY to get exec engagement. Tender expected in coming 2 weeks

12nd Sep: Add clarification made. Tender expected by End Sep. F2F by Oct (PM & Exec sponsor)

01st Sep: Add clarification on implementation & maintenance requested. Still pending of Tender

18th Aug: Tender invitation is expected by mid Sep

01st Aug: AI clarification responded. Chasing for a F2F meeting by beg of Sep

25th Jul: Add' clarification on pricing shared + respond to AI clarification by Aug 01

21st Jul: Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01)

15th Jul: Good overview session yesterday. Customer will come back in 1 month to clarify short list

14th Jul: Financial shared on Fri11. Use cases demo on Jul14

07th Jul: Short listed. Demo by July 14

30th Jun: Demo to happen by mid-July

23rd Jun: Pending confirmation demos date.

16th Jun: RFI responses sent on Jun12. Use case demo under scheduling by beg of July.

09th Jun: RFI for CLM received. To be responded by Jun12. Flanking approach on bundling both eAuction & CLM opportunities.

19th May: Demo well received. Ask for a first $ estimation.

28th Apr: eAuction demo by May 13. NDA signature going on.

18th Apr: eAuction/ Sourcing Demo expected by May02. Process on going

10th Apr: NDA to be signed. Validate eAuction POC feasibility (paid POC). POC to be delivered in coming 2 months.

04th Apr: Meeting by Apr 10 to clarify use case around eAuction and next steps.

03rd Mar: Focus on migrating some Oracle EBS proc modules this year. In parallel, they want/must start with GenAI use case. Working with Arcblue on "BPR". Invitation to Horizon. Call back in 1 month.

17th Feb: F2F meeting on Feb 27 to align on next steps

27th Jan: Request for a F2F meeting by end of Feb

17th Dec: Qualification & Overview demo. Still early stage. Expect to meet in HK by end of Jan.

ai_critical_signals.text: MTR's tender advisory partner EY has been steering toward iCertis since late 2025, and Zycus is working the issue through its Alliance partnership with no confirmed turnaround yet.
MTR's procurement team broke a month of silence and replied by email the day after the deal owner followed up on the newly issued RFP.
MTR asked for a first dollar estimate over a year ago and still has not received a quantified savings case from Zycus.
MTR's Procurement Transformation Centre of Excellence head was pulled into the tender process this spring but has never met directly with Zycus.
Procurement has asked for a dedicated risk-management review ahead of the RFP, a session that is still outstanding.

customer_preference.evidence: MTR's engagement in the 13 May 2025 eAuction demo was operational/functional ('Can we change it to auction on the second one or third one beyond?'), not evaluative preference language favoring Zycus over a rival.

ai_deal_scores_evidence.summary: MTR issued its CLM RFP on 9 Jun 2026 and Carl Kimball's follow-up drew a reply from MTR's procurement team on 6 Jul 2026, but EY, the tender's advisory partner, has pushed for iCertis since Nov 2025 and neither Alex Gautier (the mapped decision maker) nor an internal champion has ever engaged Zycus directly.

deal_risk.text: No champion or executive sponsor has been identified in 18 months of engagement, leaving Zycus without an internal advocate against EY's iCertis recommendation.

The 18 Dec 2026 close date requires RFP scoring, vendor selection and contracting to complete in roughly six months from a standing start, against MTR's demonstrated pattern of multi-month gaps between milestones.

win_position.text: Zycus remains shortlisted for the CLM RFP issued 9 Jun 2026, but EY, MTR's tender advisory partner, has pushed for iCertis since 5 Nov 2025 and no internal champion has emerged to counter that recommendation.
Alex Gautier, the GM Procurement mapped as decision maker, has never attended a Zycus call across 18 months of engagement, leaving the real budget authority unreached.

deal_momentum.text: The project was frozen by MTR for internal-priority reasons around 4 May 2026 and only resumed when the RFP was issued 9 Jun 2026, with MTR's own log rating confidence at just 30%.
MTR's procurement team broke a month of silence by replying to Carl Kimball's outreach on 6 Jul 2026, a day after he chased the project lead following the RFP release.

customer_commitment.text: MTR asked Zycus for 'a first dollar estimation' after the 13 May 2025 eAuction demo, but no quantified ROI or business case has been delivered or discussed since.
